2026-06-11 — T1–T7 Done. Commits: 1dad80fe (T1+T2), e8993870 (T2+T3), e44c8fda (T4+T5+stale fix). R-V141P0-01 fixed. Defer-7 disposition: all waived/deferred (test-only). No schema touched → T7 no-op. AC1+AC2+AC4+AC5 verified; AC3 (spec stamp) pending PM review.

2026-06-04-v1.33-work-model-and-creator-.title: API contract tests for /v1/local/works endpoints present but not literal 6-status-code matrix per endpoint
2026-06-04-v1.33-work-model-and-creator-.note: Fix wave (95470aa) added 16 works_api.rs tests covering 200/201/401/404 + creator isolation + idempotency replay. MISSING literal 400/409 asserts (409 not needed: race resolves to 200; 400 not yet for PATCH enum). T1 §7.5 said cover 200/201/400/401/404/409; coverage is strong but not literal 6/endpoint. CLOSE-as-fixed for 4 Critical (R-03 original = 'no tests'); keep this entry open for the literal-matrix gap.

2026-06-04-v1.33-work-model-and-creator-.title: system preset list logic inlined in system/mod.rs (151-line edit) and only surfaces embedded presets
2026-06-04-v1.33-work-model-and-creator-.source: qc2 F-008 (re-confirmed in wave 2; also qc1 S-001)
2026-06-04-v1.33-work-model-and-creator-.scope: crates/nexus42/src/commands/system/mod.rs (list_system_presets); T1 contract §7.3 named crates/nexus42/src/commands/system/preset.rs as a new file
2026-06-04-v1.33-work-model-and-creator-.note: T1 contract specified new file crates/nexus42/src/commands/system/preset.rs; actual change inlined into system/mod.rs. list_system_presets labels everything 'embedded' even though /v1/local/presets returns embedded+system+user. Fix: refactor to specified file layout + make list merge all 3 groups.

2026-06-04-v1.33-work-model-and-creator-.title: nexus-local-db::works uses runtime sqlx::query for static DML, bypassing compile-time contract
2026-06-04-v1.33-work-model-and-creator-.source: qc1 W-002 (re-confirmed in wave 2; explicitly deferred in plan §8)
2026-06-04-v1.33-work-model-and-creator-.scope: crates/nexus-local-db/src/works.rs (entire file); nexus-local-db/AGENTS.md, nexus-daemon-runtime/AGENTS.md require compile-time checked queries
2026-06-04-v1.33-work-model-and-creator-.note: Static INSERT/SELECT/UPDATE/DELETE use sqlx::query / query_as (runtime) with `// SAFETY:` comments. Conflicts with crate AGENTS.md mandatory compile-time macros. Deferred in P1 fix wave (requires DATABASE_URL workflow for cargo sqlx prepare). Spot check in wave 2 confirmed no SQL injection vectors (only bound params after constant column lists).

v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.title: Keyword LIKE pattern doesn't escape special characters
v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Add escape_sql_like helper for user-supplied keyword values.

v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.title: Missing prompt injection queue cleanup — table growth
v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Add daemon maintenance cleanup for expired/consumed rows.

v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.title: JSON LIKE query performance on memory_fragments degrades with scale
v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Pre-release volumes low. Consider FTS5 or keyword index at scale.

v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.title: parse_judge_response keyword matching can produce false positives from LLM output
v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.note: GO/NOGO word list matches substrings in LLM output. Consider regex anchors or parse first word of response.

v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.title: memory-augmented preset exit_when kind:rule without rule expression — may need explicit always_true
v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.note: P2 resolved: exit_when kind:rule is explicit always-true, locked by test memory_augmented_rule_exit_is_explicit_always_true. Loader treats empty rule as always-true (correct for this preset). Documented in YAML comments and embedded-presets/README.md.

v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.title: SessionCapture RwLock uses write() for all accesses — cosmetic under single-threaded IPC
v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Benign in current single-threaded IPC model. Revisit if worker becomes concurrent. Consider parking_lot::RwLock.

v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.title: JobLifecycleGuard FSM — no RAII guard to ensure mark_done/mark_failed called exactly once
v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Mark_done now runs before KeyBlock insert (W-001 fix), reducing risk. Full guard would eliminate the class.

v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.title: Drop timeout 150ms may be insufficient for heavy LLM children — make configurable
v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.note: PID existence check added (C-4 fix). Configurable timeout via TimeoutConfig would improve safety.

v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.title: LIST_BY_WORLD_LIMIT=500 silently truncates — no truncation indicator to caller
v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Pre-release volumes well under 500. Add total_count or has_more field when scale requires it.

v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.title: claim_job re-fetches row after commit solely for started_at timestamp
v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Extra DB round-trip for started_at. Could compute in Rust post-commit. Low priority optimization.

v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.title: insert_with_retry returns generic Configuration error on collision — should use dedicated type
v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.note: UUIDv4 makes collision near-impossible. Error type improvement for API clarity.

v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.title: Dynamic SQL (format!) in reference_source::list and kb_store::list_by_world — not parameterized
v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Limit/offset now clamped (C-2 fix). format! needed because sqlx offline mode cannot bind LIMIT/OFFSET. Document exception.

v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.title: Extraction prompt format!() doubles memory for large work entries
v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Typical work entries are small structured summaries. Streaming approach if large-file support added.

v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.title: sqlx prepare CI enforcement — ensure offline metadata stays green after query changes
v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.note: .sqlx/ metadata churn in diff expected after query changes. Add CI step to verify sqlx prepare is current.

2026-06-04-v1.34-fl-e-run-intents-and-st.note: sqlx tracking prevents normal double-run; non-blocking safety add.

2026-06-04-v1.34-fl-e-run-intents-and-st.note: Function defined but no caller after refactor; remove or wire in P2/P4.

2026-06-04-v1.34-fl-e-run-intents-and-st.note: Non-blocking; stage list returns minimal info; extend in V1.34+.

2026-06-04-v1.34-fl-e-run-intents-and-st.note: Filename date format vs existing convention; non-blocking.

2026-06-04-v1.34-fl-e-run-intents-and-st.note: Dedicated /v1/local/works/{id}/stage/advance endpoint would clarify semantics; non-blocking.

2026-06-04-v1.34-agent-tool-implementati.title: P4 agent tool production caller wiring (orchestration -> dispatch_from_worker)
2026-06-04-v1.34-agent-tool-implementati.note: V1.35 P0 assessed: adapter exists, but production wiring requires IPC-layer changes (IpcClient notification handling, WorkerHandleProvider trait extension, worker-side tool request forwarding). Non-surgical across 3+ crates — deferred to V1.36. V1.35 ships dispatch_from_worker adapter + tests only.

2026-06-07-v1.37-novel-foundation-first.title: novel-writing preset.yaml had gates at YAML top-level instead of under preset: — gates silently ignored before fix
2026-06-07-v1.37-novel-foundation-first.source: QC1/QC2/QC3 fix wave — discovered during gate_failure_returns_422 test debugging
2026-06-07-v1.37-novel-foundation-first.note: Root cause fixed in this commit (gates moved under preset: key). Residual tracks the broader concern: serde silently ignores unknown top-level keys in YAML, so misplaced sections cause no error. Consider adding a strict-mode serde guard or loader validation that warns on unknown top-level keys in preset.yaml.

2026-06-08-v1.38-multi-chapter-selection.title: next_chapter() selection race window under concurrent creator run continue (single-user assumption)
2026-06-08-v1.38-multi-chapter-selection.scope: crates/nexus-local-db/src/work_chapters.rs:511-530 (next_chapter); callers in daemon get_work enrichment + CLI continue path
2026-06-08-v1.38-multi-chapter-selection.note: next_chapter() runs three SQL queries (now consolidated to one MIN query after F-001) without transaction/claim atomicity. Two concurrent creator run continue calls could both observe the same chapter before any transition has flipped its row to draft/finalized. Per spec §4.5.2 the query is the SSOT; novel.chapter_transition guards against from_status mismatch but the user may see duplicate in-progress schedules. Single-user local-first path is safe; race only under concurrent invocation. Document single-writer assumption or add try_claim_next_chapter (UPDATE ... RETURNING chapter) in a follow-up if multi-terminal use is common.
2026-06-08-v1.38-multi-chapter-selection.closure_note: V1.39 P5 accept-with-doc: local-first single-writer invariant. Doc on next_chapter() captures invariant + upgrade pattern. Commit 932097ea.

2026-06-08-v1.38-multi-chapter-selection.title: Plan T9 surface of on-disk missing-file hints only partially delivered in CLI status
2026-06-08-v1.38-multi-chapter-selection.note: Status surface shows DB rows + progress + next-action hint + comment acknowledging on-disk absence, but no explicit on-disk missing-file warning line (e.g. body_path absent for chNN). DB SSOT preserved; reconcile-chapters covers remediation. Add explicit check-and-emit in status arm in a follow-up.
2026-06-08-v1.38-multi-chapter-selection.closure_note: V1.39 P5 accept: CLI surface of on-disk missing-file hints out of P5 scope. Register R-V139P5-N1 for V1.40 UX follow-up.

2026-06-08-v1.38-multi-chapter-selection.title: Write-on-read anti-pattern in GET /v1/local/works/{id} (lazy completion promotion)
2026-06-08-v1.38-multi-chapter-selection.note: GET /v1/local/works/{id} mutates works.status to completed when is_work_completed() returns true. Violates REST semantics; two identical GET requests can return different status values. The handler comment acknowledges this as lazy promotion matching V1.36 P4 fix wave auto-promote pattern. Move promotion to finalize transition path or document lazy promotion explicitly in API contract.
2026-06-08-v1.38-multi-chapter-selection.closure_note: V1.39 P5 accept-with-doc + idempotency test. Lazy completion-promotion is intentional, now contractual. Commit 63b6ad59.

2026-06-08-v1.38-multi-chapter-selection.title: WorkApiDto.chapters vector size uncapped for unusual total_planned_chapters
2026-06-08-v1.38-multi-chapter-selection.note: list_chapters returns all rows with no limit. At typical novel scales (<=100 chapters) this is fine; not a practical concern since total_planned_chapters is user-controlled at init and local DB is not exposed to untrusted network clients. Add LIMIT or pagination in a follow-up if multi-volume works with100+ chapters become common.
2026-06-08-v1.38-multi-chapter-selection.closure_note: V1.39 P5 accept: chapters uncapped is theoretical DoS for local-first. Register R-V139P5-N2 for V1.40 UX.

2026-06-08-v1.38-multi-chapter-selection.title: is_work_completed total_planned_chapters=NULL explicit test missing
2026-06-08-v1.38-multi-chapter-selection.note: Code path returns false early for NULL or0 total_planned_chapters; doc comment correct. Add explicit test case to make the §6.1 guard behavior self-documenting.

2026-06-08-v1.38-novel-writing-parameter.title: Pre-existing latent: stage_advance for produce creates schedule with empty chapter fields when next_chapter=None (novel-completion); surfaced by P1 fields but not introduced by them
2026-06-08-v1.38-novel-writing-parameter.note: When next_chapter returns None (novel-completion per P0 selection), stage_advance for produce target still builds WorkFields with all chapter fields None and creates a schedule. The preset gates do not reject it. Does not regress completion or auto-enqueue (DF-53 deferred). Document that the schedule will be created but the writer prompt will see missing chapter context and should no-op or surface completion. Surface as completion UX in V1.39+.

2026-06-08-v1.38-novel-writing-parameter.title: Frontmatter field documentation removed from draft-chapter.md without replacement
2026-06-08-v1.38-novel-writing-parameter.note: Old template contained a bulleted list explaining each frontmatter field (title, chapter, status, word_count, world_refs). New template drops this entirely; frontmatter example block remains but field descriptions are gone. Restore compact 1-2 line summary of required frontmatter fields in a follow-up.

2026-06-08-v1.38-novel-writing-parameter.title: _deprecated/ prompt files still embedded in binary via include_dir!; not loaded but occupy space
2026-06-08-v1.38-novel-writing-parameter.source: QC1 S-2 / QC3 S2 (V1.38 P1 review); qc-consolidated.md
2026-06-08-v1.38-novel-writing-parameter.note: Quarantined draft-body.md and draft-intro.md are still embedded in the binary at compile time via include_dir!. They are not referenced by preset.yaml. Either move _deprecated/ outside embedded-presets/ entirely, or configure include_dir! to exclude _deprecated/ patterns. Not blocking for this plan.

2026-06-08-v1.38-novel-writing-parameter.title: outline_path / body_path required: true in templates with no defaults for non-CLI callers
2026-06-08-v1.38-novel-writing-parameter.note: If a caller (e.g., e2e test, daemon, or future API) constructs preset.input directly without going through CLI stage_advance, the required: true constraint means template rendering fails. Current callers all populate via stage_advance. Add runtime validation in schedule builder that warns when required template vars are missing from preset.input. Document expanded preset.input contract in preset.yaml header comments.
2026-06-08-v1.38-novel-writing-parameter.closure_note: V1.39 P5 accept: current callers all CLI flows. Register R-V139P5-N3 for V1.40 if non-CLI caller added.

2026-06-08-v1.38-novel-writing-parameter.title: chapter_label for chapter >=100 yields "100" not "0100" (no fixed-width beyond 2 digits)
2026-06-08-v1.38-novel-writing-parameter.note: Spec §4.5.6 accepts 2-digit zero-pad for chapter1-99; chapter100+ grows naturally to width. Document format contract in spec or add fixed-width if future novels require it. No correctness break for P1.

2026-06-08-v1.38-novel-writing-parameter.title: O(n) linear scan over chapters[] in stage_advance (typical n<100, negligible)
2026-06-08-v1.38-novel-writing-parameter.source: QC2 S-2 / QC3 S4 (V1.38 P1 review); qc-consolidated.md
2026-06-08-v1.38-novel-writing-parameter.note: chapters.iter().find(...) is O(n). At typical novel scales (<=100 chapters) this is negligible. Add index if multi-volume works with 100+ chapters become common.

2026-06-08-v1.38-novel-writing-parameter.title: stage_advance lacks audit logging for chapter context extraction (observability gap)
2026-06-08-v1.38-novel-writing-parameter.note: Add tracing::info! or debug! span showing the selected chapter context (chapter_label, outline_path, body_path, slug) to aid production debugging when chapter selection behaves unexpectedly.

2026-06-09-v1.39-fl-e-auto-chain-engine.title: ACH timestamp ID format %Y%m%d%H%M%S%3f lacks entropy — concurrent writers that both observe driver_schedule_id=null could collide in same millisecond
2026-06-09-v1.39-fl-e-auto-chain-engine.closure_note: V1.40 P4 T2: appended AtomicU32 monotonic counter suffix to ACH schedule ID. No new deps. Commit c47d2125.
2026-06-09-v1.39-fl-e-auto-chain-engine.note: Local-first single-user assumption lowers probability to near-zero. Fix: append ULID suffix or per-creator monotonic counter to the timestamp ID format. Helper is single source of truth (W-A) so the fix is one location.

2026-06-09-v1.39-fl-e-auto-chain-engine.title: creator run resume only clears auto_chain_interrupted; no synchronous nudge/tick/enqueue creates observable 'resume requested but chain idle' window
2026-06-09-v1.39-fl-e-auto-chain-engine.closure_note: V1.40 P4 T2: added auto_chain_interrupted to PatchWorkRequest + supervisor.tick() trigger on resume. Commit c47d2125.
2026-06-09-v1.39-fl-e-auto-chain-engine.note: Fix: after clearing the auto_chain_interrupted flag, call supervisor.tick() (or a small kick() helper) so the resumed Work progresses in the same request rather than waiting for the next cycle. Could also fold into auto_chain::enqueue_auto_chain_schedule.

2026-06-09-v1.39-fl-e-auto-chain-engine.title: tick_inner loads ALL creator_schedules rows regardless of status on every terminal event — auto-chain increases completion frequency and amplifies O(N) cost
2026-06-09-v1.39-fl-e-auto-chain-engine.closure_note: V1.40 P4 T2: scoped tick_inner SELECT to status IN ('pending','running','paused'). Both query sites converted to runtime query_as with SAFETY comments. Commit c47d2125.
2026-06-09-v1.39-fl-e-auto-chain-engine.note: Fix: scope the SELECT in tick_inner to pending|running statuses only. Auto-chain does not create new schedules with these statuses (it creates 'pending' or 'running' at enqueue, then transitions), so scope change is safe.

2026-06-09-v1.39-fl-e-auto-chain-engine.title: patch_work_stage fail-fast reorder test (W-D) exercises the atomic function in isolation, not handler-level reordering — stronger test would simulate full handler
2026-06-09-v1.39-fl-e-auto-chain-engine.source: qc3 S-1 targeted re-review (V1.39 P0); qc-consolidated.md
2026-06-09-v1.39-fl-e-auto-chain-engine.note: Atomic-function test still proves the fail-fast property. Integration test would be more thorough but adds dependency on the surrounding handler. Accept as-is; document in test docstring if helper makes it visible.

2026-06-09-v1.39-fl-e-auto-chain-engine.title: Boot-resume partial index existence verified but planner usage not empirically confirmed via EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN
2026-06-09-v1.39-fl-e-auto-chain-engine.source: qc3 S-2 targeted re-review (V1.39 P0); qc-consolidated.md
2026-06-09-v1.39-fl-e-auto-chain-engine.note: SQLite's planner may or may not use the partial index depending on table statistics; index is still safe (additive, no write overhead on paths that don't match the WHERE clause). Empirical EXPLAIN test can be added in a follow-up.

2026-06-09-v1.39-research-stage-wiring.closure_note: V1.40 P4 T5: documented run_intents impact in research preset.yaml header. Commit 9dae01f4.
2026-06-09-v1.39-research-stage-wiring.note: grep codebase for work_init references; document result.

2026-06-09-v1.39-research-stage-wiring.title: P0.5 C-1 fix introduced a security regression: gated presets could be enqueued without work_id (PR #50 cursor review medium). Closed by fix branch fix/v1.39-preset-gate-bypass.
2026-06-09-v1.39-research-stage-wiring.source: PR #50 cursor automation review (medium); follow-up to V1.39 P0.5 C-1 fix
2026-06-09-v1.39-research-stage-wiring.note: Security: when preset declares gates, work_id is now REQUIRED; resolved from input.work_id OR seed.work_id; missing work_id fails closed with 422 preset_gates_failed. Regression test gated_preset_without_work_id_is_rejected added. Commit 3cc1601f on fix/v1.39-preset-gate-bypass.
2026-06-09-v1.39-research-stage-wiring.closure_note: Closed in fix branch fix/v1.39-preset-gate-bypass commit 3cc1601f. Gate-eval now resolves work_id from input OR seed; gated presets without work_id fail closed with 422 preset_gates_failed; regression test added.
